---
title: "Notes — Fourier Transform"
date: 2026-03-26
categories: [math, signal-processing]
---

## Definition

The continuous Fourier transform of a function $f(t)$ is:

$$
\hat{f}(\xi) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f(t)\, e^{-2\pi i \xi t}\, dt
$$

And the inverse:

$$
f(t) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} \hat{f}(\xi)\, e^{2\pi i \xi t}\, d\xi
$$

---

## Key properties

| Property | Time domain | Frequency domain |
|----------|-------------|------------------|
| Linearity | $\alpha f + \beta g$ | $\alpha \hat{f} + \beta \hat{g}$ |
| Time shift | $f(t - t_0)$ | $e^{-2\pi i \xi t_0} \hat{f}(\xi)$ |
| Convolution | $(f * g)(t)$ | $\hat{f}(\xi) \cdot \hat{g}(\xi)$ |
| Parseval | $\int |f|^2\, dt$ | $\int |\hat{f}|^2\, d\xi$ |

---

## Visualisation

The animation below (rendered in Manim) shows how a square wave decomposes
into a sum of sinusoids as more Fourier terms are added:

![Fourier series decomposition](images/fourier.gif){fig-align="center" width="80%"}

::: {.callout-note}
Replace `images/fourier.gif` with your own Manim output. Any `.gif` or `.mp4`
in the `images/` folder works — no extra config needed.
:::

---

## Worked example — Gaussian

For $f(t) = e^{-\pi t^2}$, the Fourier transform is its own Gaussian:

$$
\hat{f}(\xi) = e^{-\pi \xi^2}
$$

This is the only function (up to scaling) that is an eigenfunction of the
Fourier transform with eigenvalue 1.

If we want it to have normalization one, we then need to define:
$$
\hat{f}(\xi) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2 \pi \sigma^2}} e^{-\pi \xi^2}
$$
---

## Interactive TNB Frame Animation

Enter a parametric curve $\vec{r}(t) = (x(t),\, y(t),\, z(t))$ as three
comma-separated expressions in $t$, then click **Plot**.
Use the slider to move the TNB frame along the curve.

::: {=html}
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/mathjs/13.0.0/math.min.js"></script>
<script src="https://cdn.plot.ly/plotly-2.35.2.min.js"></script>

<style>
  #tnb-input-row  { display:flex; gap:.5rem; flex-wrap:wrap; align-items:center; margin-bottom:.75rem; }
  #tnb-curve-in   { flex:1 1 300px; font-family:monospace; font-size:.9rem;
                    padding:.35rem .55rem; border:1px solid #ccc; border-radius:4px; }
  #tnb-plot-btn   { padding:.35rem 1rem; background:#0d6efd; color:#fff;
                    border:none; border-radius:4px; cursor:pointer; font-size:.9rem; }
  #tnb-err        { color:#c0392b; font-size:.88rem; margin-bottom:.5rem; display:none; }
  #tnb-slider-row { display:none; align-items:center; gap:.8rem; margin-bottom:.5rem; }
  #tnb-t-slider   { flex:1; }
  #tnb-t-label    { font-family:monospace; min-width:4ch; }
  #tnb-plot       { width:100%; height:520px; }
</style>

<div id="tnb-input-row">
  <input id="tnb-curve-in" type="text"
         value="2*cos(3*t), 2*sin(t), 2*cos(2*t)"
         placeholder="x(t), y(t), z(t)  (0 ≤ t ≤ 2π)" />
  <button id="tnb-plot-btn" onclick="plotTNB()">Plot</button>
</div>
<div id="tnb-err"></div>
<div id="tnb-slider-row">
  <span>t =</span>
  <input id="tnb-t-slider" type="range" min="0" max="1000" value="0"
         oninput="updateFrame(this.value)">
  <span id="tnb-t-label">0.00</span>
</div>
<div id="tnb-plot"></div>

<script>
const TWO_PI = 2 * Math.PI;
let _rFunc = null;

/* ── math helpers ── */
function norm3(v)       { return Math.sqrt(v[0]**2 + v[1]**2 + v[2]**2); }
function normalize3(v)  { const n = norm3(v); return n > 1e-12 ? v.map(x => x/n) : [1,0,0]; }
function cross3(a, b)   {
  return [a[1]*b[2]-a[2]*b[1], a[2]*b[0]-a[0]*b[2], a[0]*b[1]-a[1]*b[0]];
}
function splitComps(raw) {
  const parts = []; let depth = 0, cur = "";
  for (const ch of raw) {
    if      (ch === "(")              { depth++; cur += ch; }
    else if (ch === ")")              { depth--; cur += ch; }
    else if (ch === "," && depth === 0) { parts.push(cur.trim()); cur = ""; }
    else                              { cur += ch; }
  }
  if (cur.trim()) parts.push(cur.trim());
  return parts;
}

/* ── numerical TNB frame ── */
function makeR(comps) {
  return t => comps.map(expr => math.evaluate(expr, { t }));
}
function compT(r, t, h=1e-5) {
  const r1 = r(t+h), r0 = r(t-h);
  return normalize3(r1.map((v,i) => (v - r0[i]) / (2*h)));
}
function compTNB(r, t, h=1e-5) {
  const T  = compT(r, t,   h);
  const T1 = compT(r, t+h, h);
  const T0 = compT(r, t-h, h);
  const dT = T1.map((v,i) => (v - T0[i]) / (2*h));
  const Nv = normalize3(dT);
  const Bv = cross3(T, Nv);
  return { T, N: Nv, B: Bv };
}

/* ── Plotly helpers ── */
function arrowTrace(p, v, scale, color, name) {
  const end = p.map((x,i) => x + v[i]*scale);
  return {
    type:'scatter3d', mode:'lines+markers',
    x:[p[0],end[0]], y:[p[1],end[1]], z:[p[2],end[2]],
    line:{color, width:6}, marker:{size:[0,6], color},
    name, showlegend:true
  };
}

function autoScale(pts) {
  const flat = pts.flat();
  const span = Math.max(...flat) - Math.min(...flat);
  return Math.max(0.2, span * 0.12);
}

/* ── main plot function ── */
function plotTNB() {
  const errEl = document.getElementById('tnb-err');
  const raw   = document.getElementById('tnb-curve-in').value.trim();
  errEl.style.display = 'none';

  let comps;
  try {
    comps = splitComps(raw);
    if (comps.length !== 3) throw new Error('Need exactly 3 comma-separated components');
    const r = makeR(comps);
    r(0); r(0.1);   // test parse
    _rFunc = r;
  } catch(e) {
    errEl.textContent   = 'Error: ' + e.message;
    errEl.style.display = 'block';
    return;
  }

  const nPts = 300;
  const ts   = Array.from({length: nPts}, (_, i) => TWO_PI * i / (nPts - 1));
  const pts  = ts.map(t => _rFunc(t));
  const scale = autoScale(pts);

  const curveTrace = {
    type:'scatter3d', mode:'lines',
    x: pts.map(p=>p[0]), y: pts.map(p=>p[1]), z: pts.map(p=>p[2]),
    line:{color:'red', width:3}, name:'r(t)', showlegend:true
  };

  const p0 = _rFunc(0);
  const {T, N, B} = compTNB(_rFunc, 0);

  Plotly.react('tnb-plot', [
    curveTrace,
    arrowTrace(p0, T, scale, 'green',  'T'),
    arrowTrace(p0, N, scale, 'blue',   'N'),
    arrowTrace(p0, B, scale, 'purple', 'B'),
    { type:'scatter3d', mode:'markers',
      x:[p0[0]], y:[p0[1]], z:[p0[2]],
      marker:{size:5, color:'orange'}, showlegend:false }
  ], { margin:{l:0,r:0,t:20,b:0}, scene:{aspectmode:'data'}, legend:{x:0,y:1} });

  const sliderRow = document.getElementById('tnb-slider-row');
  sliderRow.style.display = 'flex';
  document.getElementById('tnb-t-slider').value = 0;
  document.getElementById('tnb-t-label').textContent = '0.00';
}

/* ── slider update ── */
function updateFrame(val) {
  if (!_rFunc) return;
  const t  = TWO_PI * val / 1000;
  document.getElementById('tnb-t-label').textContent = t.toFixed(2);

  const p      = _rFunc(t);
  const {T, N, B} = compTNB(_rFunc, t);
  const scale  = parseFloat(document.getElementById('tnb-plot')._fullLayout
                   ?.scene?.camera ? 0 : 0) || 0.3;

  // Re-read scale from first arrow trace end-point distance (robust)
  const layout = document.getElementById('tnb-plot')._fullData;
  const sc = layout ? norm3([
    layout[1].x[1]-layout[1].x[0],
    layout[1].y[1]-layout[1].y[0],
    layout[1].z[1]-layout[1].z[0]
  ]) : 0.5;

  const Tend = p.map((x,i) => x + T[i]*sc);
  const Nend = p.map((x,i) => x + N[i]*sc);
  const Bend = p.map((x,i) => x + B[i]*sc);

  Plotly.update('tnb-plot', {
    x: [[p[0],Tend[0]], [p[0],Nend[0]], [p[0],Bend[0]], [p[0]]],
    y: [[p[1],Tend[1]], [p[1],Nend[1]], [p[1],Bend[1]], [p[1]]],
    z: [[p[2],Tend[2]], [p[2],Nend[2]], [p[2],Bend[2]], [p[2]]]
  }, {}, [1, 2, 3, 4]);
}
</script>
:::

---

## Code block (optional)

You can also include executable Python code cells if you have Jupyter installed:

```{python}
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

t = np.linspace(-3, 3, 1000)
f = np.exp(-np.pi * t**2)
xi = np.fft.fftfreq(len(t), d=t[1]-t[0])
fhat = np.fft.fft(f) * (t[1]-t[0])

plt.plot(np.fft.fftshift(xi), np.abs(np.fft.fftshift(fhat)))
plt.xlabel(r"$\xi$")
plt.title("Magnitude spectrum")
plt.show()
```
